rule_applied: paper_text=(title+' '+snippet).lower(); substring match per content_word; overlap>=2 forces hit; skeptical otherwise

my_judgments.reason: No content_word substring; 'behavioral gating' is not 'context gating' or 'multi-signal gating'. Skeptical: LLM-side relevance exists but rule says no content_words = miss.
No content_word substring. Skeptical miss despite topical relevance.

disagreements_with_07.note: 07 used agent judgment to call hit despite zero content_word overlap; skeptical rule rejects.
Same: 07 agent-judgment hit with overlap 0; skeptical rejects.

summary: Agree on 7/11 papers. 4 LLM-side arxiv papers: 07 generously hit them on semantic grounds; skeptical verifier requires content_word substring presence and finds none, so misses. All 6 biology papers correctly forced-hit. Net: candidate novelty claim still weakened because biology source-domain papers all hit; the LLM-side prior art whether scored hit/miss by string rule is still topically adjacent per 07's own reasoning.
